---
title: "Plumed Beaksedge (Rhynchospora microcephala)"
slug: "rhynchospora-microcephala"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475289682/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Plumed Beaksedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475289682/medium.jpg" alt="Plumed Beaksedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264676224">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=200078)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its slender stems and compact, rounded clusters of spikelets. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to vegetation structure in wetland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/200078-Rhynchospora-microcephala)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2952)
